planned_changes.description: Decide canonical semantic score/pass outcome. If the model's semantic evaluation stands (final_score=0.6 < pass threshold 0.7), update top-level grader metadata in submission manifest to passed=false and score=0.6; otherwise document the reason for override in metadata. Ensure consistency across all grader/verifier fields.

In the workbook, add an 'Assumptions' sheet enumerating each reconstructed item (weights, metric definitions, cache-hit treatment), include a column with the exact Source_Packet cell citation or embedded packet text, and provide the precise formula or cell reference used for the reconstruction.

Add cell-level comments or adjacent provenance cells linking each scoring cell to the Assumptions sheet and Source_Packet; update 'Record_Model' and 'Option_Model' sheets to show intermediate calculations for traceability.

Update ci_policy.md: include an explicit rationale for chosen weights, an alternatives table with sensitivity of threshold crossings to weight changes, tie-breaking rules, and a short summary of changes made to the workbook (with cell citations).

Address Checks!G12 and other 'NEEDS_REVIEW' flags: either resolve ambiguity in the model and update those cells to OK, or document the reason they remain flagged and link to the Assumptions entry explaining residual risk and required expert review.

Run validation in an environment with openpyxl available; produce and attach updated validation metadata (formula_count, key_cached_values) and ensure the xlsx is parseable by the grader. Include agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json following canonical trace schema.
Produce a 1-paragraph reviewer note summarizing resolved items and exact workbook cells changed; resubmit both artifacts and updated validation metadata.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve documentation and provenance gaps (reconstructed weights, metric mappings), fix grader metadata inconsistency, make workbook cell-level provenance visible, clear Checks NEEDS_REVIEW items, and re-run validation in an environment that can parse the workbook.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Resolve documentation and provenance gaps (reconstructed weights, metric mappings), fix grader metadata inconsistency, make workbook cell-level provenance visible, clear Checks NEEDS_REVIEW items, and re-run validation in an environment that can parse the workbook.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and largely correct, but semantic gaps and metadata inconsistencies prevent full credit. Key problems: (1) reconstructed scoring weights and some metric-to-driver mappings are documented as 'needs_review' without sufficient justification or cell-level provenance, (2) the xlsx preview could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl_unavailable) so direct formula provenance could not be inspected, and (3) the grader output contains an internal inconsistency where top-level score/passed flags contradict the model's semantic final_score and pass/fail rationale. Resolve these to reach full credit.
